report.title: Continuity paste from iOS inserts sandboxed file path instead of plain text
report.problem_summary: Copying rich text from Safari on an iOS device and pasting it into VS Code on macOS results in a sandboxed file path being inserted into the editor, rather than the expected plain‑text content.
evidence: Paste results in string like "/var/folders/.../42514c636c7fe381d40102cae8877a9c82f0aeb8.rtfd"
Steps to reproduce: copy from iOS Safari → paste into VS Code on macOS (same iCloud account, continuity enabled)
All extensions disabled, yet problem persists
Environment: macOS Darwin arm64 23.5.0, VS Code Insiders 1.91.1
suggested_improvements: Verify VS Code’s paste handling for Continuity clipboard data and ensure it extracts plain text from rich‑text or RTFD payloads
Add fallback logic to strip file URLs before inserting text
Update documentation or settings to allow users to configure how Continuity data is interpreted
Reproduce in minimal environment and file a bug to macOS/VS Code teams if not resolved
possible_causes: VS Code mis‑parses the macOS Continuity clipboard payload and treats a RTFD reference as a file path
macOS Continuity provides a file URL for rich text that VS Code inadvertently inserts
Bug in VS Code’s clipboard integration layer when handling iOS clipboard data
Inadequate sanitization of pasted content from external sources
report.notes: Further investigation needed to determine if the issue is isolated to Safari or occurs with other iOS apps; consider reproducing on different macOS versions and VS Code builds.

report.title: Event listener leak in chat terminal tool progress component
report.problem_summary: The chat terminal tool progress component registers an excessive number of event listeners (over 1100) during use, indicating a memory/performance leak.
evidence: "event.ts:902 Error at xhi.create (event.ts:933:15)"
"testingExplorerView.ts:510:32"
"Listeners count: 1102+"
suggested_improvements: Ensure listeners are removed when the component is disposed or reinitialized.
Refactor event registration to use one-time listeners or a cleanup method.
Add instrumentation to log listener count during lifecycle events for debugging.
possible_causes: Listeners not being unsubscribed on component teardown.
Repeated calls to onDidChange without proper removal.
Bug in testingExplorerView.ts that creates listeners on each view refresh.
report.notes: Observed during testing of issue 210848 comment; appears in VSCode 1.112.0 on all major OSes.

title: [17f] potential listener LEAK in notebook cellToolbars.ts setupChangeListeners
raw_body: ## Error Message ```` [17f] potential listener LEAK detected, having 331 listeners already. MOST frequent listener (26): ``` ## Stack Trace ``` at (anonymous) (src/vs/base/common/event.ts:1061:14) at (anonymous) [as onDidChange] (src/vs/base/common/event.ts:1246:33) at (anonymous) [as onWillAddFirstListener] (src/vs/platform/actions/common/menuService.ts:437:0) at (anonymous) [as onDidChange] (src/vs/base/common/event.ts:1255:19) at setupChangeListeners (src/vs/workbench/contrib/notebook/browser/view/cellParts/cellToolbars.ts:231:22) at _initialize (src/vs/workbench/contrib/notebook/browser/view/cellParts/cellToolbars.ts:187:7) at didRenderCell (src/vs/workbench/contrib/notebook/browser/view/cellParts/cellToolbars.ts:204:20) at renderCell (src/vs/workbench/contrib/notebook/browser/view/cellPart.ts:95:7) at (anonymous) (src/vs/workbench/contrib/notebook/browser/view/cellPart.ts:173:31) at func (src/vs/workbench/contrib/notebook/browser/view/cellPart.ts:128:9) ```` ## Details | Property | Value | | --- | --- | | Bucket ID | [`35722d78-6d9c-7412-1df7-517603d01689`](https://errors.code.visualstudio.com/bucket/35722d78-6d9c-7412-1df7-517603d01689?product=VSCode) | | Version | 1.112.0 | | Commit | [`07ff9d61`]((insiders.vscode.dev/redacted) | | Last Seen | 2026-03-23 | | Total Hits | 1.2M | | Affected Users | 48.4K | | Platforms | Linux, Mac, Windows | | Product | VSCode | ## Analysis Listener leak in notebook cell toolbar initialization. During `setupChangeListeners` in `cellToolbars.ts`, a `menuService.onDidChange` listener is registered via `onWillAddFirstListener` but not properly disposed when cells are re-rendered. This appears to be a new error in 1.112.0 - it only appears in 1.112.0 insiders builds and 1.112.0 stable. The trend is **increasing** as it accumulates with notebook usage. Note: A similar but distinct ...[truncated]
summary: [Problem] Listener leak in notebook cell toolbars, [Symptoms] excessive listeners and memory consumption, [Environment] VS Code 1.112.0 on Linux/macOS/Windows, [Possible Category] UI / Interaction, [Problem Type] Listener Leak
decision: Action: Generate (sufficient evidence found locally, reasoning skipped)

report.title: Listener Leak in Notebook Cell Toolbars
report.problem_summary: Scrolling through notebooks in VS Code results in a rapid increase of event listeners (up to 500+), causing memory pressure, slow response, and potential crashes.
evidence: Potential listener LEAK detected, having 246 listeners already. MOST frequent listener (40): event.ts:828 Error
Call stack references notebookVisibleCellObserver.ts, notebookEditorWidget.ts, notebookViewModelImpl.ts, and notebookTextModel.ts
Similar leak observed in Code‑Insiders 1.79.0 with 508 listeners
Listeners are attached during cell rendering and execution status updates without proper cleanup
suggested_improvements: Add disposal logic in notebook cell rendering to detach listeners when a cell becomes invisible or the notebook is closed.
Use weak event listeners or a single shared event for execution status updates to reduce per‑cell registrations.
Implement a listener count audit in the notebook editor to warn when counts exceed a threshold.
Profile memory usage during notebook scroll to confirm the cleanup paths.
possible_causes: Repeated registration of onDidChangeExecution listeners for each cell without deregistration.
Missing disposal of ExecutionStatusBarItemController instances when cells are removed or hidden.
Circular references between cell observers and the notebook view model preventing garbage collection.
report.notes: Issue appears in VS Code 1.112.0 (Linux/macOS/Windows) and persists in Insiders 1.79.0. It likely affects performance on large notebooks with many cells.

title: Update button on release notes covers content
raw_body:
I clicked on the "Release Notes" button from the hover widget, which opened the release notes in my active editor. Since the width was small the "Restart to Update" button shown here covers the content. 

If we want to keep this button I suggest we shorten the text to just say "Update" and make sure it adjust dynamically with the width of the editor. Although, I don't know this is necessary if the button in the title bar will still be there! 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/20bff3e2-eb3b-4309-8625-b95efb6b0992)
summary: [Problem] Update button covers content; [Symptoms] button overlaps content when editor width is small; [Environment] VS Code release notes editor; [Possible Category] UI / Interaction; [Problem Type] Button overlap
decision: Action: Generate (sufficient evidence found locally, reasoning skipped)

report.title: Update button and toolbar overlap content in the release notes editor on narrow viewports
report.problem_summary: When the editor width is reduced, the Update button (and associated toolbar) overlaps the release notes content or placeholder text, obscuring information. This occurs in the release notes editor and also when long branch names are present and the toolbar is hovered.
evidence: Screenshot from the issue showing the button overlapping the placeholder text
Description of the button covering content when the editor width is small
Observed behavior with a long branch name and hovered toolbar
suggested_improvements: Implement responsive layout logic that collapses or hides the Update button or toolbar when viewport width falls below a threshold
Reduce the button size or switch to a compact icon-only mode on small screens
Add a tooltip or inline help instead of overlaying the content
Ensure the placeholder text or release notes content has higher z-index or is not overlapped by positioned elements
Add CSS media queries or flexbox/grid adjustments to prevent overflow and overlapping
possible_causes: Absolute or fixed positioning of the button/toolbar without consideration for available width
Lack of responsive design handling for narrow viewports
CSS overflow settings that allow elements to extend beyond container bounds
Hard-coded widths or padding values that do not adapt to dynamic content like long branch names

decision: Action: Generate (sufficient evidence found locally, reasoning skipped)

report.title: Chat status bar fails to show progress when creating a new session
report.problem_summary: When a user creates a new chat session by clicking the '+' button in an empty chat state, the status bar no longer displays the in‑progress indicator, even though a progress session is started but cannot be opened.
evidence: Fresh setup with `--transient` shows the 🐸 status bar entry correctly for responses.
Creating a new chat via '+' does not trigger the progress indicator.
Source reference: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/6f0ee8f52165de5eceb00c49c07662807bd5461b/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatStatus.ts#L157
suggested_improvements: Emit a progress event when a new chat session is instantiated.
Ensure the status bar component subscribes to that event and updates the indicator accordingly.
Add a unit test verifying that the progress indicator appears during session creation.
Document the expected behavior in the chat README.
possible_causes: Missing event emission in the chat session creation logic.
Race condition where the status bar updates before the session is fully initialized.
Incorrect binding or filtering logic in chatStatus.ts that ignores new session events.

title: Default to the settings gear upgrade flow if nav bar is disabled
raw_body:
For users that may have the title bar disabled / hidden we need a fallback flow to prompt them to upgrade. We should default to the current experience showing the badge on the settings gear icon. 

As a result we will have the main title bar flow (99% users) and fallback on setting gear icon (1%). Users who get the fallback experience should still be able to see the post upgrade widget with "what's new"

As a result we could rip out the status bar implementation and setting 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/12b7a828-7162-4972-be00-9202b5bf10cc)
summary: [Problem] Title bar disabled hides upgrade prompt, [Symptoms] badge not displayed and post-upgrade widget missing, [Environment] OS: unknown; VS Code version: unknown; hardware: unknown, [Possible Category] UI / Interaction, [Problem Type] fallback flow for upgrade
decision: Action: Generate (sufficient evidence found locally, reasoning skipped)

}

title: Copilot CLI : MCP tools not honored in Custom Agents
raw_body: When the Copilot CLI runs as a third-party agent inside VS Code, custom agents invoked as sub-agents do not receive the tools declared in their `.agent.md` definition files. The agents are restricted to a limited read-only set (file viewing only), regardless of what the `tools` property specifies. The same agents work correctly when the CLI runs natively in the terminal, where they receive full access to their declared tools including GitHub MCP tools and terminal execution. ## Steps to reproduce 1. Define a custom agent in `~/.copilot/agents/` with specific tools declared in the YAML frontmatter (e.g., `github/search_issues`, `read`). 2. Start the Copilot CLI as a third-party agent inside VS Code. 3. Ask the CLI to delegate a task to the custom agent. 4. Observe that the sub-agent reports having only file-viewing tools (grep, glob, view) and cannot access its declared tools. 5. Run the same agent from the CLI natively in the terminal. The agent receives all declared tools and works as expected. ## Additional testing - Removing the `tools` property entirely (which per the [custom agents configuration docs](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/custom-agents-configuration#tools) enables ...[truncated]
summary: [Problem] Custom agents sub-agents lose declared tools when run as third-party agents inside VS Code; [Symptoms] sub-agent reports only file-viewing tools, ignoring tools property; [Environment] VS Code Insiders 1.113.0-insider on Linux x64 (WSL2); [Possible Category] Extensions; [Problem Type] Tool Access Restriction
decision: Action: Generate (sufficient evidence found locally, reasoning skipped)

report.title: Sub‑agents miss declared tools (e.g., `agent` tool) when spawned via third‑party agents
report.problem_summary: When a custom agent creates a sub‑agent (via the `runSubAgent` tool or the `agent` tool), the new sub‑agent reports only a limited set of file‑viewing tools and omits any tools declared in the parent agent’s `tools` list, including the `agent` tool itself. This occurs in VS Code Insiders 1.113.0‑insider on Linux/WSL2 and across multiple models and agent configurations.
evidence: Log shows `dispatcherAgent` includes `agent` tool in its `tools` array, yet the spawned `dispatcher` sub‑agent’s tool list contains only file‑viewing tools.
Test Setup 1: `/dispatch` prompt with `agent` tool creates `dispatcher` sub‑agent that loses the `agent` tool.
Test Setup 2: Directly using the `dispatcher` agent spawns a `codeSearcher` sub‑agent that also lacks the `agent` tool.
The issue persists across different VS Code Insider builds and multiple LLMs (Opus 4.5, Gemini 3 Flash, Gemini 3 Pro).
suggested_improvements: Restore correct tool inheritance for sub‑agents: ensure all tools from the parent agent are passed to the child agent’s tool registry.
Add explicit validation and logging for tool propagation when a sub‑agent is created.
Provide a configuration flag or extension setting to control whether sub‑agents inherit tools.
Re‑examine the sandboxing logic that might strip tools from third‑party agents.
possible_causes: A recent change in the agent sandbox that inadvertently removes non‑file‑viewing tools for sub‑agents.
Tool registration order issue: the parent’s tool list is not yet fully resolved before spawning the sub‑agent.
An extension bug in the VS Code API that filters tools for security reasons, mistakenly excluding the `agent` tool.
Conflict between the `runSubAgent` tool and the extension’s internal agent creation logic.
report.notes: Reproduced consistently across models and builds; likely a regression introduced in a recent extension update. Immediate attention needed for users who rely on nested agent workflows.

decision: Action: Generate (sufficient evidence found locally, reasoning skipped)

report.title: Copilot Chat fails to locate referenced notebook cell content and misroutes output to chat pane
report.problem_summary: When a user asks Copilot Chat to reference or modify code in a specific Jupyter notebook cell, the extension either shows the content in the chat pane instead of the notebook and searches the entire notebook rather than the target cell, producing incorrect or missing results.
evidence: Log snippet showing Copilot reading from an incorrect VS Code resource path (vscode-file://vscode-app/.../workbench.html) instead of the notebook file.
Screenshot reference indicating the content appears in the chat pane.
Reported behavior persists across multiple notebook sizes (180 KB example) and Copilot Chat versions (0.27.2).
Logs demonstrate repeated attempts to read large line ranges (0‑101, 102‑201, 751‑1001) without focusing on the cell containing the requested code.
Environment: Linux x64, VS Code 1.112.0, Ubuntu 24.04, Claude Sonnet 4 model.
suggested_improvements: Update Copilot Chat to correctly resolve the notebook file path when parsing cell content.
Implement cell‑level context extraction so that requests for code in a specific cell trigger targeted reads rather than full‑notebook scans.
Add a UI indication of the cell location when the chat outputs code, to avoid confusion with the chat pane.
Provide a diagnostic option that logs the resolved notebook path and the cell ranges being examined for easier debugging.
possible_causes: A bug in the path resolution logic that mistakenly references VS Code internal resources instead of the notebook file.
The current algorithm for determining relevant cell context may incorrectly default to searching the entire notebook.
Compatibility issue between the Copilot Chat extension version 0.27.2 and VS Code 1.112.0 or newer.
Insufficient handling of multi‑cell notebooks where the requested cell is deep within the file.
report.notes: The issue appears reproducible on Ubuntu 24.04 with VS Code 1.112.0 and Copilot Chat 0.27.2. It affects all models (Claude Sonnet 4, GPT‑4, etc.) and occurs in Agent mode. A fix should ensure proper cell context routing and accurate file path handling.

}

title: UNRESPONSIVE extension host: github.copilot-chat took 98% of 4948ms causing other extension to hang
raw_body: When having copilot and Modus toolbox extensions both installed, at times when using the copilot chat extension for a while, when reloading Window and starting the Modus Toolbox extension, it will hang when trying to do node-fetches due to copilot hogging all resources - Copilot Chat Extension Version: V0.38.2 ModusToolbox Assistant V2.27.0 - VS Code Version: 1.120.0 - OS Version: Win11 - Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Agent - Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Claude Opus 4.5 - Logs: [exthost-7078e9.cpuprofile](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26223503/exthost-7078e9.cpuprofile) Steps to Reproduce: 1. Have Copilot extension enabled and chat open 2. Download ModusToolbox Assistant Extension 3. Reload Window 4. When looking at the create project tab, extension will hang on waiting for manifest data 5. Open developer tools and see message: WARN UNRESPONSIVE extension host: 'github.copilot-chat' took 98.6255229725248% of 4948.508ms, saved PROFILE here: 'file:///c%3A/Users/soleman/AppData/Local/Temp/exthost-fee884.cpuprofile' [vscode-app-1774377494880.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26223610/vscode-app-1774377494880.log) [exthost-7078e9.cpuprofile](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26223613/exthost-7078e9.cpuprofile) I also disabled all extensions except for copilot and reloaded the window and still saw the same issue as seen in the developer tools. Here is the .cpuprofile when only copilot extension is running: [exthost-781698.cpuprofile](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26226298/exthost-781698.cpuprofile)
summary: [Problem] Unresponsive extension host due to github.copilot-chat hogging CPU, [Symptoms] causes other extensions like Modus Toolbox Assistant to hang while fetching manifest data and shows high CPU usage warnings, [Environment] Windows 11, VS Code 1.120.0, [Possible Category] Performance, [Problem Type] Extension resource hogging.
decision: Action: Generate (sufficient evidence found locally, reasoning skipped)

report.title: Unresponsive extension host caused by GitHub Copilot Chat CPU hogging
report.problem_summary: When GitHub Copilot Chat is active, the VS Code extension host becomes sluggish, causing typing, saving, and UI lag; the issue is accompanied by a high CPU usage warning and an uncaught error reported in the Running Extensions panel.
evidence: GitHub Copilot Chat activation time ~2000–2300 ms
Uncaught error shown in Developer: Show Running Extensions
High CPU usage warnings, CPU usage spikes when Copilot Chat is enabled
Disabling Copilot Chat restores normal performance
Issue reproducible on Windows 11 with VS Code 1.120.0, even with minimal extensions
suggested_improvements: Upgrade to the latest Copilot Chat version if available
Disable or uninstall Copilot Chat to confirm causality
Check the Copilot Chat error log in the Output panel for detailed stack traces
Limit the number of concurrent requests or reduce background activity via Copilot settings
Report the error to the Copilot extension maintainers with reproduction steps
Consider disabling "Run all extensions on startup" in settings.json to reduce load
possible_causes: Internal loop or blocking operation in Copilot Chat code
Unhandled exception causing the extension host to pause
Excessive background token generation or request polling
Memory leak or resource exhaustion in Copilot Chat
report.notes: The problem does not occur when all extensions are disabled, confirming it is specific to the Copilot Chat extension. The issue appears stable across recent VS Code releases and may be mitigated by disabling or updating the extension.

decision: Action: Generate (sufficient evidence found locally, reasoning skipped)

report.title: "Terminal is awaiting input" does not trigger for background terminal processes
report.problem_summary: When a shell command that requires user input (e.g., a `read` prompt) is launched in the background, VS Code’s terminal does not display the "Terminal is awaiting input" notification, even though it appears for the same command in the foreground.
evidence: Screenshot of background `read` command without notification: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/850aa79c-6648-4c0e-9d11-c14462135461
Description of background vs. foreground behavior in the issue statement
Reference to TPI #290583
suggested_improvements: Extend the awaiting‑input detection logic to monitor background processes, not just the foreground shell.
Trigger the notification when any process in the terminal session is blocked on standard input, regardless of its process group.
Provide an optional setting to enable or disable awaiting‑input for background tasks.
Add diagnostics to log when a background process is detected to be awaiting input, aiding future debugging.
possible_causes: The current implementation checks only the foreground shell’s process group for blocking I/O.
Background jobs are launched with a different session ID, so VS Code’s input monitor misses them.
The terminal integration may rely on pty status that is not updated for background jobs.
The `read` command may not send the expected signals to the VS Code terminal when run in the background.
report.notes: This issue is tracked in TPI #290583 and is relevant for all VS Code Insider builds, including 1.113.0-insider on macOS.

report.title: onDidChangeVisibleTextEditors fires too many times during integration tests
report.problem_summary: During VS Code API integration tests, the `onDidChangeVisibleTextEditors` event is emitted three times instead of the expected single time, causing an assertion failure (3 !== 1).
evidence: AssertionError at extensions\vscode-api-tests\out\singlefolder-tests\window.test.js:88:16
Build logs: https://dev.azure.com/monacotools/Monaco/_build/results?buildId=322802&view=logs&j=0304f796-6596-5b42-8573-3d5da85bc4a5&t=3408b3dd-e53f-56a6-90e3-02cabcda26b7
Build logs: https://dev.azure.com/monacotools/Monaco/_build/results?buildId=322845&view=logs&j=0304f796-6596-5b42-8573-3d5da85bc4a5&t=3408b3dd-e53f-56a6-90e3-02cabcda26b7
Build logs: https://dev.azure.com/monacotools/Monaco/_build/results?buildId=322862&view=logs&j=0304f796-6596-5b42-8573-3d5da85bc4a5&t=3408b3dd-e53f-56a6-90e3-02cabcda26b7
suggested_improvements: Ensure that each test registers the `onDidChangeVisibleTextEditors` listener only once and disposes it after the test completes.
Add safeguards to prevent duplicate subscriptions when the same extension is activated multiple times in a single test run.
Add a counter or log for subscription creation to detect and warn about unintended multiple registrations.
Run the integration test with the `--force` flag to clean the workspace state between tests.
possible_causes: Duplicate listener registration due to repeated activation of the API extension within the same test session.
Listeners not being disposed properly after each test, causing cumulative event emissions.
Multiple windows or editors opened during the test, each triggering the event separately.
report.notes: The issue appears to be isolated to the integration test environment and does not affect normal VS Code usage. A quick check of the test harness for proper disposal logic should resolve the failure.

summary: [Problem] Claude agent panel lacks title bar until reopened, breaking navigation. [Symptoms] No title bar visible on first open. [Environment] VS Code Insiders 1.113.0 on macOS 13.3 (Darwin arm64 25.3.0) with Apple M4 Pro. [Possible Category] UI / Interaction. [Problem Type] Missing UI element (title bar).
decision: Action: Generate (sufficient evidence found locally, reasoning skipped)

report.title: Claude agent panel missing title bar on first open
report.problem_summary: When the Claude agent panel is opened for the first time, its title bar is not rendered, preventing navigation until the panel is closed and reopened.
evidence: First open of the panel shows no title bar in VS Code Insiders 1.113.0 on macOS 13.3 (Apple M4 Pro).
Reopening the panel restores the title bar.
Issue appears even with all extensions disabled.
Similar native title bar visibility bug reported since 1.101 indicates a broader UI rendering issue.
suggested_improvements: Add an explicit initialization step for the panel’s title bar in the opening logic.
Verify the panel’s DOM is fully mounted before applying the title bar styling.
Implement a fallback rendering of the title bar when the initial render is delayed or fails.
Add diagnostics to log missing title bar events to aid future debugging.
possible_causes: Timing issue in panel lifecycle where title bar component mounts after the panel container is displayed.
CSS or style conflicts preventing the title bar from being visible on first render.
Bug in the panel view provider that conditionally hides the title bar based on state that isn't set until after first paint.
Interaction with native title bar settings causing a rendering race condition.
report.notes: This issue breaks usability for users navigating the Claude agent. Ensuring the title bar is present from the first render will improve navigation and consistency with other panels.

summary: [Problem] Right arrow key press initially ignored after opening model picker UI, [Symptoms] no effect on first press, [Environment] unknown, [Possible Category] UI / Interaction, [Problem Type] Key press ignored
decision: Action: Generate (sufficient evidence found locally, reasoning skipped)

report.title: Model picker keyboard interaction fails – right arrow ignored & cannot open via enter/space
report.problem_summary: When the model picker UI is focused, the first right‑arrow key press is ignored and pressing enter or space does not open the dropdown, breaking keyboard navigation and accessibility.
evidence: Right arrow key press initially ignored after opening model picker UI
Tab to model picker → press enter or space → nothing happens
Suggested patch: extend BaseDropdown with logic from dropdown.ts (lines 78‑89)
suggested_improvements: Extend BaseDropdown to include the missing keyboard handling logic from the referenced lines in dropdown.ts.
Ensure the dropdown element receives focus and the keydown events are correctly processed.
Add unit tests to cover enter, space, and arrow key navigation for the model picker.
Validate that the dropdown can be opened and navigated solely via keyboard in all supported platforms.
possible_causes: Missing or incorrect event listeners for keydown on the dropdown component.
Focus not being set on the dropdown input when the UI opens, causing the first arrow key to be ignored.
Override of the default BaseDropdown behavior that unintentionally disables enter/space opening logic.
report.notes: This appears to be a regression affecting keyboard interaction; confirming the issue in both insiders and stable builds will help prioritize the fix.

title: Image Carousel assumes only one image attachment per chat message, for Add Element to Chat
raw_body: - VS Code Version: 1.113.0-insider (Universal) - OS Version: Darwin arm64 25.3.0 Steps to Reproduce: 1. In Integrated Browser, navigate to a page. For example, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tabby_cat 2. Use the Add Element to Chat tool multiple times to attach multiple different element screenshots to the same chat message 3. Click on one of the screenshots. 4. The Carousel will only show the first image, not all of them.
summary: [Problem] Image Carousel shows only the first attached image, [Symptoms] only the first screenshot is displayed in the carousel, [Environment] VS Code 1.113.0-insider on macOS (Darwin arm64 25.3.0), [Possible Category] UI / Interaction, [Problem Type] Feature/Behavior
decision: Action: Generate (sufficient evidence found locally, reasoning skipped)

report.title: Image carousel shows only the first screenshot or displays input elements incorrectly
report.problem_summary: In the integrated browser chat, the image carousel displays only the first attached image. When elements are added to chat, they appear in the carousel but are rendered as broken input elements rather than images, causing missing or malformed screenshots.
evidence: VS Code version 1.113.0-insider on macOS (Darwin arm64 25.3.0)
Screenshot URLs: https://cleanshot.com/share/4Tk0ynGs
Reported symptom: only first screenshot displayed, subsequent images omitted or shown as broken inputs
suggested_improvements: Update carousel rendering logic to iterate over all image nodes and render each snapshot correctly.
Add a filter to exclude non‑image elements (e.g., input nodes) from the carousel display.
Implement a graceful fallback or placeholder when an image fails to load.
Add unit and integration tests for carousel rendering with multiple images.
Investigate and resolve any CSS/DOM overflow or index handling bugs that limit the carousel to a single image.
possible_causes: Bug in the carousel component that stops after rendering the first image node.
Incorrect filtering of child nodes, causing input elements to be misinterpreted as images.
CSS overflow or layout constraint that hides additional images.
State initialization that only keeps the first image in the image list.

title: When I just have a single session running, it never is "unread"
raw_body:
Testing #303345

1. Have no unread sessions
2. Open a new chat and send off a prompt
3. Go back to the sessions list view (narrow side bar so no chat is shown)
4. When the chat finishes, it doesn't become unread
summary: [Problem] Unread status not updated for single session, [Symptoms] Chat remains read after completion, [Environment] unknown, [Possible Category] UI / Interaction, [Problem Type] Unread flag not set
decision: Action: Generate (sufficient evidence found locally, reasoning skipped)

report.title: Unread flag for single chat sessions remains unset after completion
report.problem_summary: Chat sessions that are read by the user are sometimes displayed as unread after restarting VS Code Insiders, indicating that the unread state is not reliably updated or persisted.
evidence: Description of unread status not updating after chat completion.
Screenshot showing local unread sessions appearing even after all sessions have been read.
suggested_improvements: Ensure the unread flag is updated immediately when a session is marked as read.
Force a UI refresh of the chat agent view after read status changes.
Persist read status to disk or backend before exiting the application.
Add diagnostic logs for unread state transitions to aid in reproduction.
possible_causes: Race condition between UI update and state persistence.
Caching of read/unread status that is not invalidated on session close.
Incorrect handling of local session metadata during reload.
Delay or failure in syncing read state with the backend.
report.notes: Environment specifics are unknown, but issue occurs in Insiders builds. Reproducing requires restarting after reading all sessions.

report.title: Copilot agent prematurely reports command completion in VS Code integrated terminal
report.problem_summary: The Copilot agent declares a long‑running command finished after roughly 10 seconds, even though the shell still shows the command executing and the expected output is missing.
evidence: - Log shows start time Mon Aug 4 17:46:07 and agent reports success after ~10 s while `npm run lint` continues to run
- Missing ESLint/remark output and the intended completion echo `✅ Lint completed successfully`
- Reproduced with both bash 5.2.15 and zsh 5.9 on macOS 25.3.0; VS Code Insiders 1.113.0 and Copilot 1.352.1715
suggested_improvements: - Use VS Code shell‑integration completion signals instead of a fixed timeout
- Expose the timeout duration as a user‑configurable setting
- Log exit code and stdout/stderr when the terminal reports command exit
- Add a diagnostic flag to surface when the agent times out before the shell reports completion
possible_causes: - Hardcoded 10 s timeout in the Copilot agent’s command‑execution logic
- Missed or delayed shell‑integration completion events for long‑running processes
- Agent incorrectly interprets the presence of the `COMMAND_COMPLETED` signal
- Shell integration might be mis‑configured or not fully loaded in the terminal
report.notes: This issue affects build, lint, and test workflows and makes the Copilot agent unreliable for commands that exceed the current timeout threshold.

title: `auxiliaryBarFocus` is always `false`
raw_body: <!-- Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template --> <!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ --> <!-- Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions --> <!-- Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. --> <!-- Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ --> <!-- Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. --> In the current [VSCode documentation](https://code.visualstudio.com/api/references/when-clause-contexts#visible-view-container-when-clause-context) there is indication for the flag `auxiliaryBarFocus` which should be true when the auxiliary bar is on focus, false otherwise. This flag is actually always `false`. For instance, i tried to map a unique key combination: ```json { "key": "cmd+shift+9", "command": "workbench.action.previousAuxiliaryBarView", "when": "auxiliaryBarFocus", }, ``` to check that it actually works, but when i'm on focus and i hit this shortcut, nothing happens. To make sure that the shortcut would actually do something, I changed the when condition to `"when": "!editorFocus && !terminalFocus && !sideBarFocus"` and then it worked. I've found the culprit by running an inspection in VSCode and, ...[truncated]
summary: [Problem] auxiliaryBarFocus flag always false; [Symptoms] keybindings with that context never trigger; [Environment] macOS 26.3.1 VS Code 1.111.0; [Possible Category] UI / Interaction; [Problem Type] context key bug
decision: Action: Generate (sufficient evidence found locally, reasoning skipped)

report.title: auxiliaryBarFocus context key always false in auxiliary windows, preventing sidebar keybindings from firing
report.problem_summary: In VS Code, the `auxiliaryBarFocus` context key never becomes true when the user is in an auxiliary window, causing all sidebar‑related keybindings to be ignored. Sidebar views are displayed but remain unfocused, requiring a manual Alt+Tab to the main window to interact with them.
evidence: auxiliaryBarFocus flag always false
keybindings that rely on that context never trigger
macOS 26.3.1 VS Code 1.111.0
Aux window: focused window does not change to main window when focusing sidebar
suggested_improvements: Update the context key logic so that `auxiliaryBarFocus` is set to true when an auxiliary window has focus and a sidebar view is revealed.
Automatically shift focus to the main window when a sidebar view receives focus from an auxiliary window.
Add tests to validate `auxiliaryBarFocus` behavior across all window types.
Provide a setting to toggle whether focus should move to the main window for sidebar actions.
possible_causes: Incorrect or missing update to the `auxiliaryBarFocus` context key in the focus management code.
Missing focus event handling for auxiliary windows on macOS.
Bug in the command/keyboard binding engine that fails to evaluate the context correctly for aux windows.
report.notes: This issue hampers keyboard‑centric workflows and affects productivity on macOS. A prompt resolution would benefit users relying on multi‑window setups.
